zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Strickland於大約1365年出生在英國維斯特莫藍德洛福黑 卡素時，她的父親William Strickland37歲，母親Isabel Warcop36歲。她於大約1381年與Sir John Derwentwater在英國維斯特莫藍德希爾 霍爾結婚。她去世於1449年7月25日在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德洛福黑 卡素享年85歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Strickland于大约1365年出生在英国維斯特莫藍德洛福黑 卡素时，她的父亲William Strickland37岁，母亲Isabel Warcop36岁。她于大约1381年与Sir John Derwentwater在英国維斯特莫藍德希爾 霍爾结婚。她去世于1449年7月25日在英国英格兰維斯特莫藍德洛福黑 卡素享年85岁。

nameMeanings.name: Strickland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from Great and Little Strickland (Westmorland) or Strickland Ketel and Strickland Roger (also Westmorland). The placenames derive from Old English stīrc ‘stirk, young bullock or heifer’ + land ‘land’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Strickland was born about 1365, in Lowther Castle, Westmorland, England, her father, William Strickland, was 37 and her mother, Isabel Warcop, was 36. She married Sir John Derwentwater about 1381, in Sizergh Hall, Westmorland, England. She died on 25 July 1449, in Lowther Castle, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 85.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Strickland est née environ 1365, à Lowther Castle, Westmorland, Angleterre, son père, William Strickland, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Isabel Warcop, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Derwentwater environ 1381, à Sizergh Hall, Westmorland, Angleterre. Elle est décédée le 25 juillet 1449, à Lowther Castle, Westmorland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 85 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Strickland(이)가 대략 1365년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 로터 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Strickland(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Warcop(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 대략 1381년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 시즈르그h 홀에서 Sir John Derwentwater(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1449년 7월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 로터 카스틀에서 85세의 나이로 사망했다.
